evidence.observation: The claymore uses a long, broad blade and compact crossguard, creating a heavy contour while retaining the diamond material colors.
The rapier uses the same material family with a narrow blade and curved guard, producing a distinct light silhouette.

observations.statement: Width distribution, blade length, and guard mass distinguish weapon families before surface decoration is considered.
Stable material hues remain recognizable across strongly different weapon proportions.
Small highlight runs reinforce the weapon axis; darker outlines separate thin shapes from the inventory background.

derivedRules.statement: Validate that the outer contour and functional zones identify the equipment archetype at native inventory scale.
Treat palette and silhouette as independent controls so one material can support a coherent equipment family.
Confine accent colors to guards, edges, fasteners, or motifs instead of replacing the dominant material ramp.
